---
title: "Scatter Plot Widget Roadmap"
format: html
---

# ScatterPlotWidget Development Roadmap

This document tracks remaining development tasks for the Scatter Plot Widget. For documentation
of completed features and current architecture, see the [main developer documentation](index.qmd).

**Not yet implemented**: Stale key handling (Phase 6.3).

---

## Phase 7: External Feature Coloring (Widget Integration)

**Status**: Complete. All widget integration steps finished.

**Goal**: Color scatter points by an external data source (e.g., velocity colored by acceleration,
points colored by whether they fall inside a DigitalIntervalSeries interval).

### Completed Infrastructure

The shared `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` library is fully implemented and tested (24 test cases,
74 assertions). See the [FeatureColor module docs](../../../coreplotting/feature_color.qmd) for
the full API reference. Key components:

- `FeatureColorSourceDescriptor` — serializable pointer to a DataManager key + optional column
- `resolveFeatureColors()` — per-point float lookup via ATS, TensorData (TFI hash join / ordinal positional), or DIS containment
- `checkFeatureColorCompatibility()` — validates key existence, column existence, row-type compatibility
- `applyFeatureColorsToScene()` — mutates `RenderableGlyphBatch::colors[]` via `ContinuousMapping` (colormap) or `ThresholdMapping` (binary)
- `computeEffectiveColorRange()` — Auto/Manual/Symmetric vmin/vmax from resolved values
- `PlotSourceRowType` — shared enum (replaces the earlier `ScatterSourceRowType`)

### Rendering Pipeline

Feature coloring is a **post-`buildScatterPoints()` color override** inserted before group colors:

```
buildScatterPoints()          → ScatterPointData with time_indices
    → addGlyphs()             → uniform color from GlyphStyleData
    → applyFeatureColorsToScene()  → per-point color override (NEW)
    → applyGroupColorsToScene()    → group colors override feature colors
    → uploadScene()
```

No changes needed to `SceneBuilder`, `GlyphRenderer`, or shaders. Color priority (highest wins):
navigate highlight > selected > group > **feature** > default glyph style.

### Remaining Implementation Steps

| Step | Component | Location | Description |
|------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| **7.1** | `ScatterColorConfigData` | `ScatterPlotWidget/Core/` | ✅ Done |
| **7.2** | Properties panel UI | `ScatterPlotWidget/UI/` | ✅ Done |
| **7.3** | Rendering integration | `ScatterPlotWidget/Rendering/` | ✅ Done |
| **7.4** | Tooltip extension | `ScatterPlotWidget/Rendering/` | ✅ Done |
| **7.5** | Unit tests | `tests/ScatterPlotWidget/` | ✅ Done |

---

#### 7.1 `ScatterColorConfigData`

Create `ScatterPlotWidget/Core/ScatterColorConfig.hpp`:

```cpp
struct ScatterColorConfigData {
    std::string color_mode = "fixed";              ///< "fixed" or "by_feature"

    std::optional<FeatureColorSourceDescriptor> color_source;

    std::string mapping_mode = "continuous";        ///< "continuous" or "threshold"

    // Continuous mode
    std::string colormap_preset = "viridis";
    ColorRangeConfig color_range;

    // Threshold mode
    double threshold = 0.5;
    std::string above_color = "#FF4444";
    std::string below_color = "#3388FF";
    float above_alpha = 0.9f;
    float below_alpha = 0.9f;
};
```

Add `ScatterColorConfigData color_config` to `ScatterPlotStateData`.

Add to `ScatterPlotState`: `colorConfigChanged()` signal, `colorConfig()` getter,
`setColorConfig()` setter. Connect the signal to `_scene_dirty = true` in
`ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget::setState()`.

#### 7.2 Properties Panel: "Point Coloring" Section

Add a collapsible `Section` to `ScatterPlotPropertiesWidget` between "Glyph Options" and
"Selection Mode":

```
┌─ Point Coloring ─────────────────────────────┐
│  Color Mode:  [ Fixed ▾ ]                    │  ← "Fixed" / "By Feature"
│                                               │
│  ── (visible only when "By Feature") ──       │
│  Data Key:    [ velocity ▾ ]                  │  ← DM keys (ATS, TensorData, DIS)
│  Column:      [ col_0 ▾ ]                     │  ← TensorData only; hidden for ATS/DIS
│  Status:      ✓ Compatible (ATS→ATS join)     │  ← checkFeatureColorCompatibility() result
│                                               │
│  Mapping:     [ Continuous ▾ ]                │  ← "Continuous" / "Threshold"
│                                               │
│  ── Continuous mode ─────────────────────     │
│  [ColormapControls widget]                    │  ← Reuse existing ColormapControls
│                                               │
│  ── Threshold mode ──────────────────────     │
│  Threshold:   [ 0.500    ]                    │
│  Above color: [■ #FF4444 ]                    │  ← QColorDialog
│  Below color: [■ #3388FF ]                    │  ← QColorDialog
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Behaviors**:

- Data Key combo lists all DM keys of type ATS, TensorData, or DIS
- Column combo shown only for TensorData keys; hidden otherwise
- Selecting a `DigitalIntervalSeries` key auto-switches to Threshold mode (threshold=0.5)
- Compatibility label updates dynamically on key/column change

#### 7.3 Rendering Integration

In `ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget::rebuildScene()`, after `builder.build()` and before
`applyGroupColorsToScene()`:

```cpp
auto const & cc = _state->colorConfig();
if (cc.color_mode == "by_feature" && cc.color_source.has_value()) {
    auto const feature_values = resolveFeatureColors(
        *_data_manager, *cc.color_source,
        _scatter_data.time_indices, _scatter_data.time_frame);

    FeatureColorMapping mapping;
    if (cc.mapping_mode == "threshold") {
        mapping = ThresholdMapping{
            static_cast<float>(cc.threshold),
            hexColorToVec4(cc.above_color, cc.above_alpha),
            hexColorToVec4(cc.below_color, cc.below_alpha)};
    } else {
        auto const range = computeEffectiveColorRange(feature_values, cc.color_range);
        if (range.has_value()) {
            auto const preset = colormapPresetFromString(cc.colormap_preset);
            mapping = ContinuousMapping{preset, range->first, range->second};
        }
    }

    applyFeatureColorsToScene(*_scene, feature_values, mapping, point_count);
}

applyGroupColorsToScene();  // Group colors override feature colors
```

Cache `feature_values` as a member for tooltip access (step 7.4).

#### 7.4 Tooltip Extension

When feature coloring is active, append the feature value to the hover tooltip:

```
X: 1.234  Y: 5.678
TFI: 42
Color: 0.891 (velocity)           ← continuous mode
```

```
X: 1.234  Y: 5.678
TFI: 42
Color: 25.3 (velocity) [above]    ← threshold mode
```

Extend the `PlotTooltipManager` text provider to query the cached `feature_values` vector.

---

### Design Decisions

1. **Post-build color override**: Feature colors mutate `RenderableGlyphBatch::colors[]` after
   `addGlyphs()`, matching the existing `applyGroupColorsToScene()` pattern. No `SceneBuilder`
   or `GlyphRenderer` changes needed.

2. **Threshold mode as generalized binary coloring**: A single threshold mapping handles DIS
   (0/1 at threshold=0.5), binary classifier output, and continuous cutoffs (e.g., "velocity > 20").
   It is a peer of continuous colormap mode, not a special case.

3. **Shared `CorePlotting/FeatureColor/` library**: Resolution and rendering logic is shared with
   [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) (Phase 5) and
   [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) (Phase 3). Each widget provides its own
   `time_indices` and integrates the color config into its `EditorState`.

---

## Other Remaining Work

| Item | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Phase 6.3** | Stale key handling for color source (clear `color_source` when DM key is deleted) |

---

## Phase 8: SVG Export — **Complete**

**Status**: **Complete.** End-to-end export (state background color, `exportToSVG()`, properties
button, `handleExportSVG()`, registration wiring) is implemented; manual validation in the running
app has been performed. Uses `PlottingSVG` (same stack as
[EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd) and [DataViewer_Widget](../../DataViewer_Widget/index.qmd)).

**Goal**: Export the current scatter plot view to SVG for publication-quality figures.

| Step | Description | Status |
|------|-------------|--------|
| 8.1 | `background_color` in state + OpenGL clear from state | **Done** |
| 8.2 | `exportToSVG()` on `ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget` | **Done** |
| 8.3 | Export button + `exportSVGRequested` in properties | **Done** |
| 8.4 | `handleExportSVG()` on `ScatterPlotWidget` | **Done** |
| 8.5 | Wire signals in `ScatterPlotWidgetRegistration` | **Done** |
| 8.6 | Manual validation | **Done** |

**Verified:** Project build; scatter plot `ctest` targets; interactive export exercised in the app
(**8.6** checklist below).

### Architecture Assessment

The ScatterPlotWidget rendering pipeline is **the most compatible** of all remaining widgets:

- It already caches a `RenderableScene` as `_scene` (`std::unique_ptr<CorePlotting::RenderableScene>`)
- The scene contains `RenderableGlyphBatch` entries (circles, squares, crosses for scatter points)
  and optionally a `RenderablePolyLineBatch` (reference line). Both are fully supported by
  `SVGGlyphRenderer` and `SVGPolyLineRenderer`.
- Feature colors and group colors are pre-applied to `RenderableGlyphBatch::colors[]` during
  `rebuildScene()`, so the SVG renderer sees the final per-point colors with no additional work.

**Background color**: `ScatterPlotStateData` includes `background_color` (default `#1A1A1A`,
serializable). `ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget` uses `updateBackgroundColor()` (`CoreUtilities::hexToRGB`)
in `initializeGL()` and `paintGL()`, and listens for `backgroundColorChanged()`. A properties-panel
color control (like EventPlot) is optional and not required for SVG export.

### Step 8.1: Add `background_color` to `ScatterPlotStateData` — **Complete**

**Implemented in:**

- `src/WhiskerToolbox/Plots/ScatterPlotWidget/Core/ScatterPlotState.hpp` — on
  `ScatterPlotStateData`: `std::string background_color = "#1A1A1A"`; on `ScatterPlotState`:
  `getBackgroundColor()` / `setBackgroundColor()` and `backgroundColorChanged(QString)` signal
- `src/WhiskerToolbox/Plots/ScatterPlotWidget/Core/ScatterPlotState.cpp` — setter implementation
- `src/WhiskerToolbox/Plots/ScatterPlotWidget/Rendering/ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget.cpp` —
  `updateBackgroundColor()`, called from `initializeGL()` and `paintGL()`; connection to
  `backgroundColorChanged` in `setState()`

**Verify:** Project build; scatter plot tests pass.

### Step 8.2: Add `exportToSVG()` to `ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget` — **Complete**

Follows the [EventPlotOpenGLWidget pattern](../../PlottingSVG/roadmap.md). The scatter plot
caches `_scene` as `std::unique_ptr<RenderableScene>`; live view/projection matrices are copied
onto the scene before export (the OpenGL path passes matrices into `SceneRenderer::render()`,
so the CPU scene would otherwise keep identity defaults).

**Declaration:** `[[nodiscard]] QString exportToSVG();` (non-`const` — mutates scene matrices for export).

**Implementation (reference):**
```cpp
QString ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget::exportToSVG() {
    if (!_scene) {
        return {};
    }
    if (_scene->glyph_batches.empty() && _scene->poly_line_batches.empty() &&
        _scene->rectangle_batches.empty()) {
        return {};
    }

    _scene->view_matrix = _view_matrix;
    _scene->projection_matrix = _projection_matrix;

    PlottingSVG::SVGSceneRenderer renderer;
    renderer.setScene(*_scene);
    renderer.setCanvasSize(_widget_width, _widget_height);

    if (_state) {
        renderer.setBackgroundColor(_state->getBackgroundColor().toStdString());
    }

    return QString::fromStdString(renderer.render());
}
```

**Implemented in:**
- `Rendering/ScatterPlotOpenGLWidget.hpp` / `.cpp` — `exportToSVG()`, `#include <QString>`,
  `PlottingSVG/SVGSceneRenderer.hpp`
- `CMakeLists.txt` — `target_link_libraries(ScatterPlotWidget PRIVATE PlottingSVG)`

**Verify:** Project build; scatter plot tests pass.

### Step 8.3: Add `exportSVGRequested` signal to `ScatterPlotPropertiesWidget` — **Complete**

Collapsible **Export** section with **Export SVG...** button (`QSizePolicy::Expanding` width),
inserted before the bottom spacer; `clicked` connected to `exportSVGRequested()`.

**Implemented in:** `UI/ScatterPlotPropertiesWidget.hpp` (signal + `_createExportUI()`),
`UI/ScatterPlotPropertiesWidget.cpp`.

**Verify:** Project build; button visible in properties; scatter plot tests pass.

### Step 8.4: Add `handleExportSVG()` to `ScatterPlotWidget` — **Complete**

Same pattern as [EventPlotWidget::handleExportSVG()](../../../../src/WhiskerToolbox/Plots/EventPlotWidget/UI/EventPlotWidget.cpp):
`AppFileDialog::getSaveFileName` (id `export_scatter_plot_svg`), `_opengl_widget->exportToSVG()`,
`QMessageBox` on empty scene / I/O failure / success.

**Implemented in:**
- `UI/ScatterPlotWidget.hpp` — `public slots: void handleExportSVG();`
- `UI/ScatterPlotWidget.cpp` — implementation; includes `StateManagement/AppFileDialog.hpp`,
  `QFile`, `QMessageBox`, `QTextStream`
- `CMakeLists.txt` — `target_link_libraries(ScatterPlotWidget PRIVATE WhiskerToolbox::StateManagement)`

**Verify:** Project build; scatter plot tests pass.

### Step 8.5: Wire signals in `ScatterPlotWidgetRegistration` — **Complete**

In `create_editor_custom`, after `props->setPlotWidget(view)`:

```cpp
QObject::connect(props, &ScatterPlotPropertiesWidget::exportSVGRequested,
                 view, &ScatterPlotWidget::handleExportSVG);
```

**Implemented in:** `ScatterPlotWidgetRegistration.cpp`. Also removed an unused `registry` capture
from the `create_view` lambda (was triggering `-Wunused-lambda-capture`).

**Verify:** Project build; scatter plot tests pass; in the app, **Export SVG...** opens the save
dialog and produces SVG when the plot has geometry.

### Step 8.6: Manual validation — **Complete**

Checklist used for sign-off (spot-check as needed for regressions):

1. Load a dataset with scatter-compatible data (e.g., TensorData or paired AnalogTimeSeries)
2. Open a ScatterPlotWidget, configure X/Y data sources
3. Test with:
   - Default uniform glyph coloring (circles)
   - Feature coloring (continuous colormap mode)
   - Feature coloring (threshold mode)
   - Group coloring (color-by-group)
   - Reference line enabled
   - Point selection (polygon selection or single-click)
4. Click "Export SVG" in the properties panel
5. Open the resulting SVG in a web browser and/or Inkscape
6. Verify:
   - Glyph positions match on-screen points
   - Per-point colors (feature/group) are correct
   - Selected points have highlight color
   - Reference line rendered as `<polyline>` (if enabled)
   - Background color is correct
   - Zoom/pan state is reflected

### Design Notes

- **No PlottingSVG extensions needed**: `RenderableGlyphBatch` (circles, squares, crosses,
  ticks) and `RenderablePolyLineBatch` (reference line) are both fully supported.
- **Selection highlights included**: Selected and navigated-highlight glyphs are separate
  `GlyphBatch` entries in the scene with their own colors/sizes, so they export automatically.
- **Polygon selection overlay NOT exported**: The `PolygonInteractionController` preview is
  rendered via `PreviewRenderer` (not part of the `RenderableScene`), so the in-progress
  polygon outline will not appear in the SVG. This is desirable — the polygon is transient
  interaction state, not a data visualization element.

---

# See Also

- [ScatterPlotWidget Documentation](index.qmd) — Full architecture and feature documentation
- [FeatureColor Module Docs](../../../coreplotting/feature_color.qmd) — Shared library API reference
- [Plot Widget Guide](../plot_widget_guide.md) — Shared architecture, axis types, DataManager integration
- [LinePlotWidget Roadmap](../LinePlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Feature coloring (Phase 3)
- [EventPlotWidget Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Feature coloring/sorting (Phase 5)
- [HeatmapWidget Roadmap](../HeatmapWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Colormap infrastructure (Phase 2)
